def solve(nums1: list[int]) -> bool:
    n = len(nums1)

    def check(target_parity: int) -> bool:
        """
        Checks if it's possible to make all elements in nums2 have the target_parity (0 for even, 1 for odd).
        """
        for i in range(n):
            p_i = nums1[i] % 2
            
            # Check Choice 1: nums2[i] = nums1[i]
            if p_i == target_parity:
                continue
            
            # If Choice 1 fails, check Choice 2: nums2[i] = nums1[i] - nums1[j], j != i
            # We need (p_i - p_j) % 2 == target_parity
            # This means p_i XOR p_j == target_parity
            # So, p_j must be p_i XOR target_parity
            
            required_p_j = p_i ^ target_parity
            
            choice2_possible = False
            for j in range(n):
                if i == j:
                    continue
                
                p_j = nums1[j] % 2
                
                if p_j == required_p_j:
                    choice2_possible = True
                    break
            
            if not choice2_possible:
                # If neither choice 1 nor any choice 2 works for this index i, 
                # this target parity is impossible.
                return False
                
        # If all indices can satisfy the target parity, it is possible.
        return True

    # Check if all elements can be made even (P=0) OR all elements can be made odd (P=1)
    return check(0) or check(1)
